tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: plan_100 = _make_plan(pr_identifier="100", title="Refactor auth", body="body A") plan_200 = _make_plan(pr_identifier="200", title="Add dark mode", body="body B")
plan_100 = _make_plan(pr_identifier="100", title="[erk-plan] Refactor auth", body="body A") plan_200 = _make_plan(pr_identifier="200", title="[erk-plan] Add dark mode", body="body B")

session.user:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.
Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user invoked `/erk:plan-implement` to implement Plan #9135 ("Remove `erk-plan` label"). The plan removes the `erk-plan` GitHub label from the erk project, replacing all label-based plan identification with `[erk-plan]` title prefix checks. This simplifies the label scheme from 4 labels (erk-pr, erk-plan, erk-core, erk-learn) to 3 labels (erk-pr, erk-core, erk-learn). The plan was fetched from the existing impl directory at `.erk/impl-context/plnd--remove-erk-plan-label-03-10-0509/plan.md`.
2. Key Technical Concepts: - GitHub label-based filtering vs title prefix-based identification for plan PRs - `[erk-plan]` title prefix applied via `get_title_tag_from_labels()` in `plan_utils.py` - `erk-core` label as the broader label capturing both plans and code PRs - `PlannedPRBackend.list_plans()` now filters by title prefix instead of label - `PlannedPRPlanListService` uses HTTP REST API with `FakeHttpClient` for mock URL matching - Metadata block key `ERK_PLAN = "erk-plan"` in `BlockKeys` is intentionally KEPT (it's a metadata key, not a label) - Client-side title prefix filtering added where `erk-core` label returns broader results than old `erk-plan` label - Plan validation in `prepare_plan_for_worktree()` now checks `plan.title.startswith("[erk-plan]")` instead of `"erk-plan" in plan.labels`
3. Files and Code Sections:
**Source files modified:**
- `src/erk/cli/constants.py` - Removed `ERK_PLAN_LABEL = "erk-plan"` constant (line 4) - Kept `ERK_PLAN_TITLE_PREFIX = "[erk-plan] "` (already existed)
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/objective_issues.py` - Removed `_LABEL_ERK_PLAN`, `_LABEL_ERK_PLAN_DESC`, `_LABEL_ERK_PLAN_COLOR` constants - Removed erk-plan from `_ensure_labels_exist()`, `get_erk_label_definitions()`, `get_required_erk_labels()`
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_save.py` - Removed `labels.append("erk-plan")` from label building (kept `erk-core`)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/create_cmd.py` - Changed `labels = ["erk-pr", "erk-plan", "erk-core"]` to `labels = ["erk-pr", "erk-core"]`
- `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_remote_dispatch.py` - Changed labels tuple and validation_results key from `has_erk_plan_label` to `has_erk_plan_title`
- `src/erk/cli/commands/consolidate_learn_plans_dispatch.py` - Changed labels tuple from `("erk-pr", "erk-plan", "erk-learn")` to `("erk-pr", "erk-learn")` - Changed validation_results key
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/create_pr_from_session.py` - Changed labels from `["erk-pr", "erk-plan", "erk-core"]` to `["erk-pr", "erk-core"]`
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_workflow.py` - Key change: `if "erk-plan" not in plan.labels` → `if not plan.title.startswith("[erk-plan]")` - Updated error message to reference title prefix
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py` - Changed import from `ERK_PLAN_LABEL` to `ERK_PLAN_TITLE_PREFIX` - Two validation functions updated: `validate_planned_pr` and `validate_planned_pr_remote` - Both now check `title.startswith(ERK_PLAN_TITLE_PREFIX)` instead of label membership - Updated all `has_erk_plan_label` to `has_erk_plan_title` in validation_results - Updated docstrings
- `src/erk/cli/commands/implement.py` - Changed label check to title prefix: `if not plan.title.startswith("[erk-plan]")` - Updated help text
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_plan_setup.py` - Changed local constant from `ERK_PLAN_LABEL = "erk-plan"` to `ERK_PLAN_TITLE_PREFIX = "[erk-plan]"` - Changed check from `if ERK_PLAN_LABEL in issue.labels` to `if issue.title.startswith(ERK_PLAN_TITLE_PREFIX)`
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_plans_for_objective.py` - Changed `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` to `labels=["erk-core"]`
- `src/erk/cli/commands/wt/delete_cmd.py` - Changed `PlanQuery(labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"])` to `PlanQuery(labels=["erk-core"])`
- `src/erk/cli/commands/run/list_cmd.py` - Changed `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` to `labels=["erk-core"]`
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/duplicate_check_cmd.py` - Changed labels to `["erk-core"]` - Added client-side title prefix filter: ```python existing_plans = [p for p in plan_data.plans if p.title.startswith("[erk-plan]")] ```
- `src/erk/tui/data/types.py` - Changed `PlanFilters.default()` labels from `("erk-plan",)` to `("erk-core",)`
- `src/erk/tui/data/real_provider.py` - Changed all `labels=("erk-plan",)` to `labels=("erk-core",)`
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/planned_pr.py` (CRITICAL FILE) - Changed `_PLAN_LABEL = "erk-plan"` to `_PLAN_TITLE_PREFIX = "[erk-plan]"` - In `list_plans()`: removed `all_labels = [_PLAN_LABEL]` prepend, changed to `all_labels = list(query.labels) if query.labels is not None else []` - Added title prefix filtering in the PR iteration loop: ```python if pr_info.title is None or not pr_info.title.startswith(_PLAN_TITLE_PREFIX): continue ```
- `docs/learned/planning/label-scheme.md` - Completely rewritten to remove erk-plan label references
- `src/erk/cli/commands/init/main.py` - Updated user-facing label messages
- Various docstring updates in `abc.py`, `plan_list_service.py`, `create_plan_draft_pr.py`
**Test files modified (key ones):**
- ~90 test files bulk-updated by subagent (label list changes) - `tests/unit/shared/test_plan_workflow.py` - Updated `_make_plan` default title to `"[erk-plan] Test Issue"`, renamed validation test - `tests/unit/cli/commands/branch/test_create_cmd.py` - All 5 Plan titles prefixed with `[erk-plan]` - `tests/commands/branch/test_checkout_cmd.py` - All 9 Plan titles prefixed with `[erk-plan]` - `tests/commands/implement/conftest.py` - Title prefixed - `tests/commands/dispatch/conftest.py` - Removed broken `ERK_PLAN_LABEL` import, updated labels - `tests/commands/pr/test_remote_paths.py` - Default title prefixed, dispatch test titles prefixed, validation test updated - `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_objective_plan_setup.py` - Title prefixed for is_plan test - `tests/unit/services/test_plan_list_service.py` - Fixed mock URL `labels=erk-pr,erk-plan` → `labels=erk-pr,erk-core` - `tests/unit/services/test_plan_list_service_http.py` - Fixed mock URLs `labels=erk-plan` → `labels=erk-core` - `tests/unit/gateways/remote_github/test_real_remote_github.py` - Fixed mock URLs - `tests/unit/plan_store/test_planned_pr_backend.py` - Title prefixed, test renamed - `tests/unit/plan_store/test_plan_backend_interface.py` - Title prefixed - `tests/commands/pr/test_duplicate_check.py` - All plan titles prefixed with `[erk-plan]`
4. Errors and fixes: - **First CI failure - Prettier formatting**: `docs/learned/planning/label-scheme.md` needed prettier formatting. Fixed by running `npx prettier --write`. - **First CI failure - Branch create tests**: Plan() fixtures in `test_create_cmd.py` had titles like `"Add feature"` without `[erk-plan]` prefix, causing validation failures. Fixed by adding `[erk-plan]` prefix to all Plan titles in that file. - **Second CI failure - plan_list_service tests**: `_setup_http_for_prs()` helper had default `labels: str = "erk-pr,erk-plan"` for mock URL matching, but tests now pass `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-core"]`. Fixed by changing default to `"erk-pr,erk-core"`. - **Second CI failure - plan_list_service_http tests**: Same mock URL mismatch pattern. Fixed by replacing all `labels=erk-plan` with `labels=erk-core` in mock URLs. - **Third CI failure - remote_github tests**: Mock URL label mismatches in `test_real_remote_github.py`. Fixed with `replace_all`. - **Third CI failure - checkout tests**: Plan() fixtures in `test_checkout_cmd.py` missing `[erk-plan]` title prefix. Fixed all 9 titles. - **Third CI failure - planned_pr_backend test**: Plan created with `title="Real Plan"` filtered out by new title prefix check in `list_plans()`. Fixed by adding prefix. - **Third CI failure - plan_backend_interface test**: Plan created with `title="Existing Plan"` filtered out. Fixed. - **Third CI failure - dispatch conftest**: Broken import of `ERK_PLAN_LABEL` from `dispatch_cmd.py` (constant was removed). Fixed by removing import and using `"erk-core"` directly. - **Third CI failure - remote dispatch tests**: Plans created without `[erk-plan]` title prefix. Fixed. - **Third CI failure - duplicate_check tests**: Plans created without `[erk-plan]` title prefix, filtered out by new client-side filter. Fixed all plan titles. - **Still unresolved from third CI**: `test_dispatch_happy_path` in consolidate_learn_plans and 3 workspace delete tests.
5. Problem Solving: - The core architectural change (label → title prefix) required understanding multiple code paths: validation (prepare_plan_for_worktree, dispatch validation), querying (list_issues, list_prs, plan_list_service), and creation (plan_save, create_cmd, etc.) - The `PullRequestInfo.title` field is `str | None`, requiring a None check in the title prefix filter in `planned_pr.py` - Mock HTTP URL matching in tests was a recurring issue because `FakeHttpClient` does exact URL matching, so any change in label parameters cascades to all mock URLs - The `PlannedPRBackend.list_plans()` had a hidden dependency where it prepended `_PLAN_LABEL` to ALL label queries — this needed to be replaced with client-side title filtering
6. All user messages: - The user invoked `/erk:plan-implement` (the initial command) - No other explicit user messages — all subsequent messages were tool results and system reminders
7. Pending Tasks: - Fix remaining test failures from third CI run: - `tests/commands/consolidate_learn_plans/test_dispatch.py::test_dispatch_happy_path` - `tests/commands/workspace/test_delete.py::test_delete_all_closes_pr_and_plan` - `tests/commands/workspace/test_delete.py::test_delete_all_shows_closed_plan_status` - `tests/commands/workspace/test_delete.py::test_delete_all_shows_actual_pr_and_plan_numbers_in_confirmation` - Run `make fast-ci` to verify all fixes and find any remaining failures - Complete plan-implement steps 9-14: signal ended, upload session, cleanup impl context, submit PR
8. Current Work: I was in the middle of fixing the 13 test failures from the third `make fast-ci` run. I had just fixed: - `test_planned_pr_backend.py` - added `[erk-plan]` title prefix - `test_plan_backend_interface.py` - added `[erk-plan]` title prefix - `test_remote_paths.py` - added `[erk-plan]` title prefixes to dispatch test plans - `test_duplicate_check.py` - added `[erk-plan]` title prefixes to all plan titles
I had NOT yet investigated or fixed: - `tests/commands/consolidate_learn_plans/test_dispatch.py::test_dispatch_happy_path` - `tests/commands/workspace/test_delete.py` (3 failing tests related to plan lookup — likely related to `PlannedPRBackend.list_plans()` title prefix filtering affecting how workspace delete finds associated plans)
9. Optional Next Step: Run `make fast-ci` again to verify the fixes made so far and identify any remaining failures. The workspace delete tests likely fail because `_find_plan_for_worktree()` in `wt/delete_cmd.py` calls `plan_store.list_plans()` which now filters by title prefix, and the test fixtures probably create plans without the `[erk-plan]` prefix. The consolidate_learn_plans dispatch test likely has a similar mock URL or label mismatch issue. After all tests pass, proceed with plan-implement steps 9-14 (signal ended, upload session, cleanup impl context, run CI, submit PR).
If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/schrockn/.claude/projects/-Users-schrockn--erk-repos-erk-worktrees-erk-slot-39/5965667b-3dd1-458c-a373-622669ee7ea9.jsonl Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.
session.tool_result:

session.assistant:
Now I see the issues. Let me fix them:
1. **consolidate_learn_plans test**: The test expects `("erk-pr", "erk-core", "erk-learn")` but source sends `("erk-pr", "erk-learn")` 2. **workspace delete tests**: Plans have title `"Implement feature"` without `[erk-plan]` prefix, so `list_plans()` filters them out

The file /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-39/tests/commands/workspace/test_delete.py has been updated. All occurrences of ' title="Implement feature",' were successfully replaced with ' title="[erk-plan] Implement feature",'.
session.assistant: Now let me run CI to verify all fixes.

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param: Run `make fast-ci` in the repo at /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-39 and report the results. If there are test failures, report the full error output for each failing test.

session.tool_result:
## Results Summary
**make fast-ci failed** with 3 linting errors and 3 test failures.
### Linting Errors (3)
All are line-too-long violations exceeding the 100-character limit:
1. **tests/commands/pr/test_duplicate_check.py:62** - 110 characters - Line includes: `_make_plan(pr_identifier="100", title="[erk-plan] Refactor auth", body="Restructure auth flow")`
2. **tests/commands/pr/test_duplicate_check.py:200** - 101 characters - Line includes: `_make_plan(pr_identifier="200", title="[erk-plan] Plan B", body="body B for checking")`
3. **tests/commands/pr/test_remote_paths.py:486** - 105 characters - Line includes: `_make_plan_issue(42, title="[erk-plan] Remote Dispatch Plan", branch_name="plnd/remote-test")`
### Test Failures (3)
All 3 failures are in duplicate-check tests in `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-39/tests/commands/pr/`:
1. **test_duplicate_check_remote_finds_duplicate** (test_remote_paths.py) - Assertion failure on output format
2. **test_duplicate_detected** (test_duplicate_check.py:116) - Expected: `#100: "Dark mode support"` - Got: `#100: "[erk-plan] Dark mode support"` - Output now includes the `[erk-plan]` label in the title display
3. **test_progress_reporting_lists_plans** (test_duplicate_check.py:284)
... [9 lines omitted] ...

122- http_client=http_client, 123- ) 124- # Filter to plans only (title prefix check) since erk-core includes code PRs too 125: existing_plans = [p for p in plan_data.plans if p.title.startswith("[erk-plan]")] 126- 127- if exclude_plan_id is not None: 128- existing_plans = [p for p in existing_plans if p.pr_identifier != exclude_plan_id]

131- user_output("No existing open plans to compare against.") 132- else: 133: user_output(f"Checking against {len(existing_plans)} open plan(s):") 134- for p in existing_plans: 135- user_output(f" #{p.pr_identifier}: {p.title}") -- 165- if recent_commits: 166- user_output( 167: f"Checking against {len(recent_commits)} recent {trunk_branch} commit(s)..." 168- ) 169- user_output("")
session.assistant:
The display code shows `p.title` directly. The tests previously had titles without `[erk-plan]` prefix, so the output matched `#100: Refactor auth`. Now that titles include the prefix, the output shows `#100: [erk-plan] Refactor auth`.
I should strip the prefix for display, since `[erk-plan]` is metadata, not meaningful to the user.

session.assistant:
The display at line 135 shows `p.title` (which includes `[erk-plan]`), and at line 152 shows `match.title`. The `match.title` comes from the Plan object, so it would also include the prefix.
The cleanest fix is to strip the prefix for display. Let me check how `match.title` gets populated.

session.assistant: The remote duplicate check test plans don't have `[erk-plan]` prefix. The `_make_plan` at line 294 creates plans that go through `_make_plan_list_service`, which uses `FakePlanListService` — this bypasses `PlannedPRBackend.list_plans()`. But the `duplicate_check_cmd.py` now filters client-side with `p.title.startswith("[erk-plan]")`. So these plans get filtered out.

session.tool_result:
Excellent! The fast-ci checks all passed. Here's a summary:
**Make fast-ci Results: PASSED**
All checks completed successfully:
1. **Lint** — All checks passed 2. **Format Check** — 1690 files already formatted 3. **Prettier Check** — All matched files use proper code style 4. **Markdown Check** — AGENTS.md standard passed; all CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md files properly paired (0 violations) 5. **Exec Reference Check** — `.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md` is up to date 6. **Type Checking (ty)** — All checks passed 7. **Unit Tests (erk)** — 5984 passed with 2 deprecation warnings (Click 9.0 compatibility) 8. **Unit Tests (erk-dev)** — 136 passed 9. **Unit Tests (erk-statusline)** — 115 passed 10. **Unit Tests (erk-mcp)** — 31 passed
There are no failures. The only items of note are 2 deprecation warnings about Click's `BaseCommand` being deprecated in Click 9.0, but these do not affect the test results. agentId: a3c5bbfc47f03a0f3 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed) <usage>total_tokens: 14732 tool_uses: 1 duration_ms: 32258</usage>
session.assistant: All 5984 tests pass. Now proceeding with the remaining plan-implement steps.
